etymology.description: semantic 卩(卩) (jié, 'kneeling person') + phonetic 兄(兄) (xiōng, 'elder brother') -> standing elder brother -> phonetic element
etymology.interpretation: The character 兌 originally depicts a person with an open mouth and an extended hand, symbolizing an elder brother who speaks or declares. It evolved to a phonetic role in other characters, such as 悅(悅) (yuè, 'delight'), where it also retains the suggestion of expression or declaration, fitting the emotional context.

examples.chinese: 他在银行兌钱(他在銀行兌錢)。
examples.english: He exchanged money at the bank.

examples.chinese: 我们可以在这里兌外币(我們可以在這裡兌外幣)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen kěyǐ zài zhèlǐ duì wàibì.
examples.english: We can exchange foreign currency here.

examples.chinese: 你需要先去柜台兌票(你需要先去櫃檯兌票)。
examples.pinyin: Nǐ xūyào xiān qù guìtái duì piào.
examples.english: You need to first go to the counter to exchange your ticket.
